mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because expanding manufacturing output signals rising corporate revenues and operating leverage.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO level at 94.1851 as of Sep 2011
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth months are not significantly greater than those following negative growth months (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, elevated labor market slack suppresses wage-driven consumption and compresses earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE > 8.5%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and UNRATE above 8.0% signals a severe output gap with binding monetary constraint, forcing extended easing expectations that compress equity risk premiums and drive positive 20-day S&P returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 0.07% and unemployment at 9.0% as of Oct 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound with high unemployment
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or a logistic regression interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) predicts positive 5-day S&P 500 returns during zero lower bound monetary regimes because demand surprises dominate risk appetite when discount rates are pinned near zero, whereas this relationship is weaker when policy rates are elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at 397666 and Fed funds at 0.07% as of Oct 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: In FEDFUNDS < 0.25% periods, RSAFS growth does not exhibit positive Rank IC with 5-day returns (IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window), or the return spread between top and bottom RSAFS growth quintiles is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in housing starts (HOUST) predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because residential investment recovery drives multiplier effects through construction, durable goods, and employment.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows Rank IC < 0.03 with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below 95, manufacturing sector contraction depresses earnings expectations and drives negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO < 95 are positive in > 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of declining retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) identifies a consumer demand collapse that is not captured by either series alone, generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of negative RSAFS growth and UNRATE > 8% fails to produce negative 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression is insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) above its 4-quarter moving average signals durable demand expansion that drives positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as corporate earnings revisions follow with a lag.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above its 4-quarter average does not coincide with positive 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year window).
